ground_truth.title: EatingWell's Top 10 Food & Nutrition Trends for 2025
ground_truth.author: Danielle DeAngelis
ground_truth.main_content: EatingWell predicts 10 food and health trends for 2025 based on collected data. The first trend is the anti-inflammatory diet, which focuses on incorporating whole foods like fruits, vegetables, and healthy fats while reducing processed foods and added sugars. Tea emerges as a major trend, with options like mushroom tea and matcha gaining popularity for their potential health benefits. Tropical fruits such as mango, guava, and passion fruit are being incorporated into more recipes beyond summer dishes. Dates are being used as a natural alternative to added sugar in recipes. Honey mustard is becoming a popular condiment beyond traditional uses. High-protein ingredients are being added to more food products, including unexpected items like high-protein bagels and yogurt. Canned foods are being recognized for their convenience and nutritional value. Cognitive health is a growing focus, with consumers seeking foods that support brain health. Non-alcoholic beverages continue to trend as more options become available for those choosing not to consume alcohol. The article was reviewed by dietitian Emily Lachtrupp, M.S., RD, ensuring accuracy of nutritional information.
with: We collected data to help us predict 10 food and health trends you'll see everywhere in 2025.
The anti-inflammatory diet is our top trend for 2025, focusing on incorporating more whole foods.
Dates are being used more frequently in place of added sugar in recipes.
High-protein ingredients are being added to more food products, including unexpected items like bagels.
Non-alcoholic beverages continue to trend as more options become available.
